title: cassia australis var. revoluta
intro: Cassia australis var. revoluta, also known as the Australian senna or small-leaf cassia, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including constipation and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and pods are rich in anthraquinone glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and provide several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Cassia australis var. revoluta can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a powder or capsule.
